mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate held above its trailing 12-month average signals restrictive monetary policy that raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of macro regime.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level deviation and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or high-rate periods show equal or higher forward returns than low-rate periods with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection into labor-market deterioration that historically precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns following a >0.3pp 3-month UNRATE rise are positive in more than 55% of triggered historical instances, or effect size is not statistically distinguishable from unconditional baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) jointly signals broad-based consumer demand contraction that is not captured by either series alone, and this joint weakening predicts negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon as earnings expectations reprice.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change < 0 AND HOUST 3-month change < 0 simultaneously
failure_condition: Joint RSAFS/HOUST contraction periods do not produce more negative 60d forward returns than periods where only one series contracts, tested via difference-in-means with p > 0.05, or interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over univariate models.

mechanism_narrative: During an industrial expansion regime, sustained positive INDPRO growth (3-month annualized rate above zero) signals rising corporate output and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon; this relationship reverses or weakens in contraction regimes.
regime_hint: industrial expansion phase of the business cycle
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth periods within confirmed expansion regimes fail to show higher mean 60d forward returns than negative growth periods (difference not significant at p < 0.05), or sign flips within the same regime across multiple cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When month-over-month CPIAUCSL growth exceeds the trailing 6-month average by more than one standard deviation, it signals an inflation surprise that raises expected future rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets rapidly reprice discount rates.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL MoM change > 1 std dev above its trailing 6-month average
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following triggered CPI surprises are not significantly more negative than unconditional 5-day returns (p > 0.05) across at least two distinct tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month momentum in PAYEMS (nonfarm payrolls) reflects ongoing labor demand strength that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue, producing modestly positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days on an always-on basis.
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS MoM change and 5d forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive PAYEMS months do not outperform negative PAYEMS months at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime where GDPC1 growth decelerates from prior quarters while remaining positive, the market anticipates an approaching peak, causing S&P 500 forward returns to be negative over a 60-day horizon as investors rotate defensively ahead of a growth slowdown.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth decelerates for 2 consecutive quarters while remaining positive
failure_condition: 60d forward returns during decelerating-but-positive GDPC1 growth periods are not significantly lower than returns during accelerating growth periods (p > 0.05), or the pattern fails to replicate across at least two business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap between FEDFUNDS and the inverse of UNRATE (proxying a Taylor-rule-like policy stance) signals monetary policy that is tighter than labor-market conditions warrant, and this joint mismatch predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days that neither series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises while UNRATE simultaneously falls or stays flat over 3 months
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with tight labor market
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-UNRATE interaction term shows no incremental predictive power over either univariate series alone (Rank IC improvement < 0.02), or 20d forward returns during joint tightening episodes are not significantly more negative than baseline (p > 0.05).
